channel.title: 文献追踪系统
channel.description: 自动追踪 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉领域最新文献

item.title: 弗洛凯时间晶体传感器中量子增强传感的最优可观测量及其应用
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 M. A. Manya, Andrei Tsypilnikov, Fernando Iemini
本文讨论了如何确定和实现能够达到量子费舍尔信息（QFI）界限的计量协议中的可行最优可观测量。研究表明，对称对数导数（SLD）算符在矩量法中可以达到QFI界限，但其复杂性限制了直接实验实现。为解决此问题，作者在弗洛凯时间晶体（FTC）交流场传感器模型中，发现SLD可以被更简单的可观测量（如裸自旋磁化或宇称可观测量）近似。这些理论预测在核磁共振FTC传感器系统中得到了验证，为量子增强传感提供了实用的方法。

item.title: 半导体激光器中复杂的频率依赖正交压缩
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Daniele Nello, Giuseppe Patera, Lorenzo Columbo
本文基于全量子朗之万方法，对量子阱激光器中的正交压缩进行了全面研究。作者计算了激光场的频率分辨压缩图，并首次揭示了半导体激光器中频率依赖压缩和复杂或隐藏压缩的存在。研究还分析了线宽增强因子在这些现象出现中的作用。这些结果确立了半导体激光器作为非经典光产生平台，并为其在量子通信和传感中的应用开辟了新前景。

item.title: 超越克利福德操作的非局域计算任务的等价性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Andreas Bluhm, Simon Höfer, Alex May, Florian Speelman, Philip Verduyn Lunel
非局域量子计算（NLQC）研究了两个协作方如何利用一次同步量子通信和共享纠缠在分布式系统上实现信道。本文继续研究NLQC任务之间的归约关系，特别关注量子位置验证中涉及大经典输入和固定大小量子输入的NLQC示例。研究发现了NLQC任务之间的许多新关系，揭示了不同任务的相对难度，并为量子位置验证方案提供了更可行的协议。

item.title: 腔辅助自旋-运动-光子相互作用的散射理论
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Seigo Kikura, Aruku Senoo, Akihisa Goban, Shinichi Sunami
腔辅助光子散射（CAPS）是实现固定量子比特内部态与飞行光子之间强相互作用的有效机制。本文针对CAPS操作中腔量子电动力学的共振耦合机制，发展了一个完整的分析框架。该框架通过将运动自由度纳入散射理论，描述了原子运动与反射光子之间的相干相互作用。由此产生的紧凑的基于算符的输入-输出关系，适用于各种腔几何、自旋依赖陷阱势和非相同多自旋系统。

item.title: 反常超辐射相的非厄米性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jun-Ling Wang, You-Qi Lu, Qing-Hu Chen, Yu-Yu Zhang
本文提出了一个厄米压缩-迪克模型，作为多体光物质系统中非厄米物理的最小设置。该模型通过厄米二次玻色子系统实现了非厄米哈密顿量。研究发现，有效的宇称-时间（PT）对称性纯粹源于压缩，并在强自旋压缩下识别出反常超辐射相的非厄米性，表现出独特的PT对称性自发破缺。这种奇异相具有复杂的激发谱，并在一个特殊点经历动力学相变。结合破缺的厄米性，人工磁场产生了具有显著量子放大效应的非互易动力学。

item.title: 用于离散变量量子信息处理的超低损耗集成光子平台
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yi-Han Luo, Ruiyang Chen, Zeying Zhong, Sanli Huang, Sicheng Zeng, Zhenyuan Shang, Yue Hu, Zhen Chen, Yuan Chen, Xue Bai, Junqiu Liu
光子集成电路为量子信息技术提供了可扩展且鲁棒的途径，但光学损耗是其扩展的关键瓶颈。本文通过展示一种单片、超低损耗的氮化硅（Si₃N₄）集成光子平台，克服了这一速率-损耗障碍。该平台专为高性能离散变量量子信息处理而设计，无缝集成了窄带光子对源、低损耗量子比特融合电路和可重构态分析干涉仪。

item.title: 见证扩展：一种用于分析和可测量混合态资源检测的统一框架
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yifan Tang, Chengkai Zhu, Yuzhen Zhang, Jens Eisert, Zi-Wen Liu, Ingo Roth, Otfried Gühne, Xin Wang, Zhenhuan Liu
量子信息科学致力于利用量子资源完成信息处理任务，并解决量子相和动力学中的基本问题。本文提出了“见证扩展”这一统一框架，用于构建检测与明确自由酉群相关的量子资源的非线性判据。这些判据适用于纯态和混合态，基于目标态的多项式函数，可通过实验估计和分析评估。该框架自然地导出了多种已知的资源检测量，为量子资源识别和检测提供了强大且实用的方法。

item.title: 基于光镊阵列中离子的量子计算机架构
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Benjamin F. Schiffer, Christopher Monroe, Peter Zoller, J. Ignacio Cirac
本文提出了一种基于光镊阵列中离子的量子计算机架构，结合了囚禁离子量子比特的长相干时间与光镊平台的可重构性和并行操作能力。研究开发并分析了由有效偶极子间库仑相互作用介导的纠缠门机制，实现了精确且温度鲁棒的质心和相对运动轨迹闭合。该架构还概述了基于态选择极化率的钡离子具体实现，并研究了并行门执行期间串扰的抑制，为可扩展的离子-光镊量子处理器建立了现实途径。

item.title: 囚禁离子系统中大规模多模纠缠门合成
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 YingYe Huang, Wentao Chen, Guoyu Zou, Xuan Fan, Jing-Ning Zhang, Kihwan Kim
囚禁离子系统是可扩展量子信息处理的领先平台，但随着离子数量增加，多模纠缠门合成变得极具挑战。本文开发了一种多频门合成的数值框架，通过交替最小化策略直接搜索满足自旋-自旋相互作用、抑制残余自旋-运动纠缠和限制实验控制资源等竞争要求的控制场。该框架提高了数值稳定性，并成功合成了多达N=1000个离子链中的全连接和最近邻相互作用模式，仅使用全局激光控制。

item.title: 量子极限亚衍射望远镜需要真正的多望远镜干涉
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yujie Zhang, Yunkai Wang, Wilson Wu, Thomas Jennewein
传统恒星干涉测量通过望远镜之间的成对互相关性重建非相干源。本文研究表明，对于通用图像矩估计，成对测量不足以实现量子极限亚衍射成像。研究推导了N望远镜阵列的图像矩量子费舍尔信息（QFI）标度，并证明仅限于望远镜对的任意测量只能达到二阶QFI标度。因此，在量子极限下估计高阶矩需要真正的多望远镜干涉，为此作者构建了阵列-SPADE测量方法以达到最优QFI标度。

item.title: 自筛选量子密钥分发
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Saman Sarshar, Mostafa Annabestani
本文提出了一种新颖的双向量子密钥分发（QKD）协议，其中发送方和接收方利用最大纠缠贝尔态的一个量子比特作为密钥交换的量子信道。该协议引入了一种基于扰码算符的新安全机制，所有筛选操作和窃听者检测均在量子通信阶段完成后由接收方独家执行。由于控制模式从未公开宣布，该协议能有效防止依赖模式相关调整的攻击，并能利用通常会被丢弃的轮次来检测窃听者的存在。

item.title: 拓扑子系统码的环面码实现：基于反交换量子自旋液体的框架
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Vaibhav Sharma, Sumiran Pujari
本文引入了一个构建拓扑子系统码的框架，该框架基于反交换量子自旋液体。这类模型可视为环面码的空间修改，其包含大量反交换的局域守恒算符，导致广泛的基态简并，这构成了相关量子纠错码中子系统自由度的基础。该码继承了量子自旋液体的多体拓扑序，使其成为拓扑子系统码，并具有大量不受校验算符干扰的局域规范量子比特。

item.title: 彩色霍夫施塔特蝴蝶作为多体量子霍尔相图
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Giovanna Marcelli, Tadahiro Miyao, Domenico Monaco, Stefan Teufel, Marius Wesle
本文证明了彩色霍夫施塔特蝴蝶对于一类广泛的弱相互作用格点费米子系统具有多体解释。研究构建了一个三维参数空间中的开放区域，其中无限体积的相互作用系统具有局域唯一的带隙基态。文中证明了一个多体带隙标记定理：宏观欧姆定律中出现的霍尔电导是常数且量子化的。因此，非相互作用霍夫施塔特蝴蝶的整数颜色作为相互作用量子霍尔相的霍尔电导标签得以保留。

item.title: 非马尔可夫原子约瑟夫森结中通过零模奇异点实现的非遍历动力学相变
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Koichiro Furutani
本文在一个首尾相连的玻色-约瑟夫森结中，展示了开放量子系统通常因环境退相干而热化并失去初始记忆的范式被打破。通过精确的非马尔可夫朗之万模拟，研究发现了一种新型的非遍历动力学相变，其转变为一种运行态，在马尔可夫极限下没有对应物。该相变由非马尔可夫摩擦引起的零模奇异点所控制，为耗散环境中的长寿命量子存储器提供了有前景的平台。

item.title: 多量子比特系统中用于暗物质探测的相干集体响应
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ryuichiro Kitano, Ryoto Takai
本文提出了一种利用N个叠加态的拉姆齐型干涉仪阵列作为探测波状暗物质的传感器。该方案的信噪比与√N α成正比，对耦合的灵敏度按δ α ~ 1/√N缩放。与需要高度纠缠量子比特的拉比型跃迁方案相比，拉姆齐型测量不需要纠缠态，允许通过简单地放置大量量子比特来显著增加N。研究表明，当N ≳ 10⁶时，其对耦合的预测灵敏度可与现有实验室、天体物理和宇宙学限制相媲美或超越。

item.title: Z₂拓扑绝缘体的几何体边对应
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Alexis Drouot, Jacob Shapiro, Xiaowen Zhu
二维费米子时间反演不变绝缘体存在两种拓扑相，由Z₂值不变量（Fu-Kane-Mele指数）表征。本文证明了弯曲界面的几何体边对应关系：如果两个此类绝缘体占据由弯曲边界分隔的互补区域，则界面系统的Z₂边缘指数是两个体Z₂指数之差与边界和测量区域相关的几何交点数的乘积（模二）。该论证是霍尔绝缘体弯曲界面连接公式的Z₂类比。

item.title: 确定性Floquet-PXP模型中的精确子系统动力学
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Katja Klobas
热力学大量子多体系统中局部子系统的动力学可被视为有效开放系统，其有效浴的作用可通过影响矩阵来表征。本文证明了确定性Floquet-PXP模型（规则201）是一个可解实例，其影响矩阵由满足有限代数条件的有限维矩阵乘积算符（MPO）给出。研究提供了该解决方案，并表征了多时间自相关函数。

item.title: 用恒定键维度的张量网络态近似局部性质
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yichen Huang
量子多体系统的经典模拟因其指数增长的希尔伯特空间而面临挑战，张量网络态是有效表示许多物理相关量子态的强大方法。本文证明，在一维系统中，Rényi纠缠熵R_α（α<1）的面积律意味着矩阵乘积态表示的键维度为poly(1/δ)。在二维系统中，R_α（α<1）的面积律意味着投影纠缠对态表示的键维度为eO⁽¹/δ⁾。这些结果严格证明了张量网络模拟中采用与系统大小无关的键维度的普遍做法是合理的。

item.title: 当量子模型与经典模型不一致时：超越最小范数最小二乘的学习
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Slimane Thabet, Léo Monbroussou, Eliott Z. Mamon, Jonas Landman
基于变分量子电路（VQCs）的量子机器学习算法是量子计算应用的重要候选。本文提出了一个框架来避免随机特征近似技术，并利用现有结果建立了量子模型权重向量的必要条件，以规避这些去量子化方法。研究表明，该理论与先前证明的离散输入量子优势兼容，并提供了连续输入的优势示例。这种分离与大权重向量范数相关，从而证明了设计量子模型以实现超越经典模型性能的可能性。

item.title: 量子置换下的不变性排除了副统计
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Manuel Mekonnen, Thomas D. Galley, Markus P. Mueller
量子系统在粒子交换下不变时，通常被认为是玻色子或费米子，尽管量子理论原则上允许更一般的置换行为。本文提出了两个独立的、与模型无关的论证，排除了副统计。首先，引入了“完全不变性”的概念，要求量子系统在置换下不仅保持其局部状态，还保持其携带的关于其他系统的量子信息。其次，要求量子系统在量子置换下不变。研究表明，只有当系统是玻色子或费米子时，这两个原则才成立。

item.title: 通过损耗信道高效分发多体纠缠的私有量子网络传感
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yoshihiro Ueda, Makoto Ishihara, Wojciech Roga, Masahiro Takeoka
量子网络传感有望将空间分布式参数函数的估计精度提升至超越散粒噪声极限，其关键资源是多体量子纠缠，并能提供隐私保护。本文分析了一种基于高效GHZ态分发方案的量子网络传感协议。与传统基于纠缠分发的方法相比，该协议显示出对分布式参数的某些函数，其由损耗引起的估计误差有所降低。此外，研究还考虑了一种场景，其中一个人可以在不泄露敏感信息的情况下估计分布式参数的线性组合。

item.title: 费米子高斯态之间平均子系统Bures距离的有效计算
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zhouhao Guo, M. A. Rajabpour, Jiaju Zhang
平均子系统迹距离被提议作为量子多体混沌和可积性的指标，但其计算面临高计算成本和本征态定义模糊的挑战。本文开发了一种高效算法来计算费米子高斯态之间的Bures距离，从而能够处理更大的系统尺寸。研究计算了自旋-1/2横场伊辛链和二次Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev模型中本征态的平均子系统Bures距离，结果与子系统尺寸线性增长的早期猜想一致。该工作表明混沌系统与可积系统中平均子系统距离的不同标度源于局部守恒量的非连续性。

item.title: 量子计算中基于离散流的生成模型用于测量优化
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Isaac L. Huidobro-Meezs, Jun Dai, Rodrigo A. Vargas-Hernández
量子模拟中的测量瓶颈限制了化学精度，需要大量测量次数。本文提出一种算法，将生成流网络（GFlowNets）应用于哈密顿量的图表示着色，以生成奖励驱动的非重叠分组。该方法通过用户定义的奖励比例采样着色图，从而整合不同的目标项以捕捉多目标权衡。在基准分子哈密顿量上的测试显示了其潜力。

item.title: 大规模局部纯化最大混合态：纠缠剪枝与对称性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Amit Jamadagni, Eugene Dumitrescu
局部纯化密度算符（LPDOs）是表示大规模混合量子态的先进张量网络方法，但其非唯一性导致表示复杂性次优。本研究通过全面的数值和分析，解决了噪声将密度算符去极化为最大混合态时的次优问题。文章分析了保真截断、等距规范变换等数值工具，并利用最大混合LPDO的注入性和对称性导出了去纠缠器的解析表达式。

item.title: 利用平移对称性实现基于压缩猫量子比特的量子计算
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Tomohiro Shitara, Gabriel Mintzer, Yuuki Tokunaga, Suguru Endo
平移对称性在玻色子量子纠错中至关重要，但压缩猫（SC）码缺乏利用其平移对称性进行量子纠错的硬件高效途径。本文展示了这种对称性为SC码的自主量子纠错和通用量子计算提供了一条实用路径。研究提出了一种QEC协议，能自主恢复因物理误差偏离码空间的状态，并分析了其弛豫速率。在此框架下，还提出了改进误差标度的逻辑态制备、逻辑门和逻辑Z读出方法。

item.title: 离散时间量子伊辛链的不可逆Kramers-Wannier对偶对称性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Akash Sinha, Pramod Padmanabhan, Vladimir Korepin
可积Trotter化方法允许离散时间演化连续时间可积多体系统并保留其守恒量。本文明确展示了临界横场伊辛模型的一阶Trotter化是可积的，并从非均匀传递矩阵中获得了离散时间守恒量。研究重点关注Trotter化演化中的不可逆Kramers-Wannier对偶对称性，发现时空离散化导致这些对偶算符加倍，并可用于不同阶Trotter化之间的映射。

item.title: 量子最大割的Lovász theta和Shearer下界
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Felix Huber
量子最大割问题在计算机科学和多体量子物理中具有重要意义。本文证明了图的量子最大割的一个下界，该下界由其补图的Lovász theta函数表示。对于具有m条边的图，给出了具体的下界表达式，并通过乘积态实现。研究还扩展了Carlson等人和Shearer的结果，为所有无三角形图的量子最大割提供了新的下界。

item.title: 无需认证和信息泄露的量子密钥分发
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zixuan Hu, Zhenyu Li
传统量子密钥分发（QKD）系统依赖认证的经典后处理通道，但QKD本身缺乏认证能力且经典后处理会引入信息泄露。本研究提出了一种新型QKD协议，该协议无需单独认证，可防止信息泄露，并显著提高可实现密钥速率。通过完全移除公共经典通道，该设计利用两个可重用预共享密钥实现了完美保密性。

item.title: 基于FPGA的用于光子量子计算应用的快速前馈系统
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Daniel Duggan, Simon Filgis, Axel B. Bregnsbo, Jürgen Saalmüller, Jonas S. Neergaard-Nielsen, Tobias Wintermantel, Ulrik L. Andersen
现场可编程门阵列（FPGA）为新兴量子和光子技术中的实时信号处理提供了高性能解决方案。本文介绍了一种基于FPGA的快速前馈系统，该系统集成了高量子效率的全光纤零差探测器，以实现连续变量（CV）测量基量子信息处理协议所需的低延迟信号处理。该系统能够实时执行信号采集、调节和逻辑操作，满足光子量子计算协议的严格延迟要求。

item.title: 用算子代数量子纠错揭示客观性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Marin Girard, Gong Cheng, ChunJun Cao
量子达尔文主义解释了客观性如何从量子力学中产生，但现有方法常未能完全捕捉其所有方面。本文将量子达尔文主义与算子代数量子纠错联系起来，表明客观性的出现可等同于量子码的代数局部可恢复性。将此代数框架应用于稳定子码，研究实现了对经典性和冗余的更精确表征，统一了传统客观性度量，并支持大规模Clifford模拟退相干动力学。

item.title: 量子近似优化和绝热优化算法中的纠缠标度与问题结构
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Georgios Arapantonis, Paraj Titum, Gregory Quiroz
纠缠被认为是量子算法能力的关键资源。本文通过量子近似优化算法（QAOA）应用于MaxCut问题，研究了纠缠与问题结构和算法性能的关系。研究发现次优变分参数训练会显著改变纠缠分布，但使用高性能优化器后，QAOA展现出与费米子高斯态一致的纠缠标度。与绝热量子计算的比较显示，其纠缠分布的标度行为与QAOA显著不同。

item.title: 无后选择多体动力学中测量诱导的量子跳跃统计交叉
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kazuki Yamamoto, Ryusuke Hamazaki
本文揭示了连续监测多体系统中量子跳跃子系统涨落的非平凡交叉现象。在弱测量强度下，涨落遵循泊松统计的体积定律；而在强测量强度下，则出现超泊松统计的反常普适标度定律。这种交叉显著影响量子跳跃速率的估计精度。研究表明，反常标度源于积分多体自相关函数，且瞬态动力学而非Liouvillian间隙对标度定律有贡献。

item.title: 费米子相互作用下自由费米子测量诱导的体积-面积定律纠缠相变
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Matthew S. Foster, Haoyu Guo, Chao-Ming Jian, Andreas W. W. Ludwig
在多体系统的体积-面积定律纠缠相变中，量子混沌被抑制。本文提出，这暗示了受监测相互作用费米子测量诱导相变（MIPT）场论中某个“质量”项的消失。研究考虑了DIII类一维受监测相互作用马约拉纳费米子的MIPT，并推测有相互作用的MIPT与无相互作用的情况相同，体积定律相通过危险的无关质量项产生。

item.title: 基于希尔伯特空间嵌入的量子最大似然预测
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sreejith Sreekumar, Nir Weinberger
最大似然预测（MLP）是现代大型语言模型的核心任务。本文研究了MLP的量子版本，通过将经验概率分布嵌入量子态并最小化量子相对熵来获得量子最大似然预测器（QMLP）。研究推导了QMLP在迹范数和量子相对熵方面的非渐近性能保证，并提供了一个统一框架来处理经典和量子LLM中的MLP。此外，还推广了量子信息投影问题和量子勾股定理。

item.title: 广义对称性下量子态可区分性的算子管区理论
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Song He
该研究在具有广义对称性（包括由融合范畴描述的不可逆情况）的多体系统中建立了量子态可区分性的变分原理。当对称性作用终止于纠缠截面时，可区分性由对称性受限测量资源理论中的边界管代数控制。文中提出了表征物理上可接受测量仪器的条件，并指出最优测量结构由边界管代数的中心唯一确定，从而优化了基于保真度和对称性分辨的描述。

item.title: 范尼尔-斯塔克-朗道局域化诱导的三维晶格中的量子霍尔效应
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 D. -H. -Minh Nguyen
该研究探讨了在平行电场和磁场作用下的立方晶格中量子霍尔效应。在量子极限下，能谱呈现出霍夫施塔特蝴蝶的3D推广形式。当费米能级位于能谱间隙内时，垂直于场的平面内的霍尔电导被量子化，并表现出受体块陈数保护的拓扑手性铰链模式。这项工作为在固态异质结构和合成晶格中研究量子霍尔效应提供了一个新颖平台。

item.title: 面内自旋旋转驱动的拓扑相变
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xinyue Zhu, Yu Xie, Yifei Hao, Fei Gao, Yue Li, Junting Zhang
该研究建立了一个对称性框架，通过连续的面内自旋旋转实现拓扑态的可逆切换，这受磁点群对贝里曲率分布的约束。以二维kagome铁磁陈绝缘体为例，作者展示了60度面内磁化旋转可以反转陈数符号，并经过拓扑平庸态。微磁模拟证实，这种自旋重定向驱动的切换在极小的磁场和超快时间尺度下即可实现，为拓扑态的操控提供了一种高效、低能耗的新范式。

item.title: 具有可调空间序的镝同位素二元偶极凝聚体
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Shenshuang Nie, Zibin Jiang, Junrong Huang, Xiao Luo, Fucheng Qin, Kaiyue Wang, Mingyang Guo
该研究在一个极简装置中实现了高磁性同位素¹⁶²Dy和¹⁶⁴Dy的二元玻色-爱因斯坦凝聚体。利用其相似的单粒子特性和可调谐的Feshbach共振，作者驱动了偶极二元凝聚体的相互作用控制混溶转变，伴随着凝聚体界面的重构。在固定相互作用下，粒子数不平衡提供了第二个控制旋钮，用于连续调节相分离序。这些结果确立了镝同位素混合物作为一种紧凑平台，用于设计偶极量子物质中的混溶性、界面和空间序。

item.title: 二维dₓ^₂₋y^₂波交错磁体中的Floquet工程陈绝缘体
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hosein Cheraghchi
该研究探讨了在圆偏振光照射下的二维dₓ^₂₋y^₂波交错磁体中的Floquet工程拓扑相。利用晶格模型和Floquet理论，作者证明了光照可以诱导可调谐的量子反常霍尔相，其陈数可达±3。研究结果通过计算反常霍尔电导和纳米带边缘模式得到验证，表明d波交错磁体是实现非平衡拓扑物态的有前景平台。

item.title: 辐照二维dₓy波交错磁体中的Floquet拓扑相和反常霍尔特征
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hosein Cheraghchi
该研究探讨了在非共振圆偏振光驱动和面外磁化作用下的二维dₓy波交错磁体中的Floquet拓扑相。利用晶格Floquet公式，作者发现系统由驱动参数β控制，该参数决定了不同能隙闭合点和相关拓扑相的出现。研究揭示了在不同β值下，系统可以实现陈数高达|C|=4的拓扑相。在金属态下，反常霍尔电导提供了这些Floquet拓扑相的实验特征。

item.title: 用于合成新型Mo(W)Te2体系的受控化学气相沉积
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ya Deng, Zi-Yi Han, Yao Wu, Kongyang Yi, Ya-Ning Ren, Dundong Yuan, Chao Zhu, Lin He, Zheng Liu
该研究提出了一种受限空间化学气相沉积（CVD）策略，用于合成高质量的Mo(W)Te2单晶、合金以及横向/垂直异质结构。这种方法通过降低生长温度窗口并结合定制的前驱体配置和分步热处理，成功克服了传统方法中结构多晶型和不稳定性问题。高分辨率STEM表征显示了原子级锐利且成分明确的无缝边界。该方法为制备清洁异质界面和建立理想的原位实验系统提供了新途径。

item.title: 二阶拓扑超导体中自旋极化的局域和非局域STM输运特征
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Paweł Szumniak, Daniel Loss, Jelena Klinovaja
该研究数值探究了二维二阶拓扑超导体（2D SOTSCs）中马约拉纳角态（MCSs）的自旋和输运性质。研究表明，MCSs具有独特的电子自旋极化空间分布，可用于标记MCSs。作者提出了一种全面的自旋分辨输运协议，用于测量这种自旋织构并检测MCSs的编织，这对于拓扑量子计算至关重要。该技术还可以用于检测更高能量准粒子态的自旋密度分布。

item.title: 扭曲双层石墨烯-WSe₂异质结构中自旋轨道耦合驱动的拓扑超导性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kamalesh Bera, Arijit Saha, Tanay Nag
该研究基于扭曲双层石墨烯（tBLG）的低能连续模型，并考虑了邻近诱导的Ising、Rashba和本征自旋轨道耦合，构建了相应的Bogoliubov-de Gennes哈密顿量。作者理论研究了拓扑超导性的出现，这种超导性可能在tBLG、铌和二硒化钨异质结构中通过实验验证。拓扑超导相具有有效的p波配对特征和反转能带色散，受体块能隙保护并由陈数拓扑表征。研究结果揭示了自旋轨道耦合对拓扑相形成的关键作用。

item.title: 掺杂石墨烯纳米带的低能模型
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 J. Ferrer, A. García-Fuente, Y. Yang, S. Volosheniuk, H. S. J. van der Zant
该文分析了化学势位于体单粒子能带内的独立掺杂石墨烯纳米带的多体行为。作者将扩展和局域Hubbard模型精确映射到包含铁磁交换和对跳跃相互作用的Kanamori模型。研究解析确定了库仑矩阵元及其随纳米带宽度和长度的标度行为。提出的低能Kanamori哈密顿量可用于研究纳米带对外部场的响应，并发现该模型和参数可以产生开壳层、高自旋多体态，从而导致壳层和自旋阻塞响应。

item.title: 超导谐振器中传播自旋波与微波光子的强耦合
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yi Li, Jinho Lim, Xingzhi Wang, Tomas Polakovic, Carissa Kiehl, Moojune Song, Phuoc Cao Van, Ralu Divan, Ulrich Welp, Charudatta Phatak, Jong-Ryul Jeong, Kab-Jin Kim, Jian-Min Zuo, Axel Hoffmann, Valentine Novosad
该研究展示了在超导谐振器-磁性薄膜混合电路中，传播自旋波模式与微波光子之间的强耦合。通过将谐振器直接制作在钇铁石榴石薄膜上，作者实现了Damon-Eshbach和后向体自旋波模式与谐振器的强耦合，耦合强度超过了磁振子和光子阻尼率。此外，研究还观察到混合磁振子模式的非互易自旋波辐射，突出了将本征自旋波非互易性引入混合磁振子系统的潜力。这些结果为自旋波磁振子学与腔磁振子学的结合以及在量子信息科学中的应用开辟了新途径。

item.title: 隐藏有序的狄拉克费米子
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Bitan Roy
该研究提出了一种洛伦兹对称狄拉克理论的厄米扩展，通过补充一个质量状反交换狄拉克算符。由此产生的理论在任何维度下都表现出标志性的线性能量-动量关系，其涌现的节点准粒子被称为“隐藏有序狄拉克费米子”。这些费米子受对称性保护，其响应类似于原始狄拉克系统，但费米速度经过重整化。文章构建了此类隐藏有序无质量狄拉克费米子的晶格模型，并概述了验证这些预测的有前景的平台。

item.title: 非厄米磁场中的狄拉克费米子：零模与指标定理
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Christopher A. Leong, Bitan Roy
该研究探讨了洛伦兹对称的非厄米狄拉克理论中，空间调制非厄米参数如何将无质量狄拉克费米子耦合为非厄米规范场。当平面狄拉克系统包含有限非厄米磁场通量时，谱中会出现一系列空间局域的左右零能量本征模。这些发现为非厄米磁催化、强耦合非厄米分数拓扑相和非厄米手征异常等未来研究提供了新的可能性。

item.title: QED_d-GNY模型和标量QED_d中的中心荷C_J和C_T
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yongwei Guo, Zhijin Li
本文计算了d维共形QED_d-Gross-Neveu-Yukawa (GNY)模型和标量QED_d中中心荷C_J和C_T的领先阶1/N修正。研究还获得了最低伴随双线性标量在一般d维下的O(1/N)阶标度维度。在d=3时，作者评估了拓扑U(1)对称电流的中心荷C_Jtop的次领先阶1/N展开。这些理论与SO(5)对称的去禁闭量子临界点(DQCP)的潜在联系是主要研究动机，并且QED₃₋GNY模型的大N预测与SO(5) DQCP的非微扰估计结果吻合良好。

item.title: 任意范围电子-声子耦合的从头算降维理论
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Norm M. Tubman, Christopher J. N. Coveney, Chih-En Hsu, Andres Montoya-Castillo, Marina R. Filip, Jeffrey B. Neaton, Zhenglu Li, Vojtech Vlcek, Antonios M. Alvertis
从头算降维方法在低能子空间内描述材料的电子结构，但通常忽略电子-声子耦合或仅考虑短程相互作用。本文提出了一种新的从头算降维理论，能够平等地考虑所有范围的电子-声子耦合机制。该计算实现与现有降维方法兼容，并应用于极性材料MgO和GeTe，揭示了短程和长程电子-声子耦合在决定电荷相互作用强度方面的重要性。

item.title: 机器学习带电胶体多体势揭示原始模型强耦合区域的气液旋节不稳定
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Thijs ter Rele, René van Roij, Marjolein Dijkstra
过去实验和模拟观察表明，高电荷胶体悬浮液中存在同电荷吸引现象，但直接模拟高电荷胶体计算成本高昂。为解决此问题，本研究利用机器学习框架构建了能准确描述胶体有效相互作用的ML势。这些ML势实现了快速模拟，并成功再现了低电荷数原始模型模拟中的气液和气固相分离。将基于ML的模拟扩展到更高价态时，也揭示了同电荷吸引和气液旋节不稳定。

item.title: d波交错磁体中光致拓扑相变与反常热输运
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ayesha Maryam, Muzamil Shah, Kashif Sabeeh, Reza Asgari
本文研究了线性偏振光驱动的二维d波交错磁拓扑绝缘体中的本征热输运和Floquet工程拓扑。通过分析反常霍尔、能斯特和热霍尔电导率及其自旋分辨对应物，并利用解析计算的高频有效哈密顿量推导出贝里曲率的闭合形式。研究表明，线性偏振光在交错磁体中打破了自旋扇区间的对称性，导致一系列自旋选择性拓扑相变，从量子自旋霍尔态到自旋极化陈绝缘体，最终到平庸相。

item.title: 均匀随机码的星积
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Johan Vester Dinesen, Ragnar Freij-Hollanti, Camilla Hollanti, Benjamin Jany, Alberto Ravagnani
本文研究了两个维度不一定相同的均匀随机线性码的星积的期望维度问题。通过星积与双线性形式评估之间的对应关系，给出了期望星积维度的明确下界。研究证明，随着域大小的增加，期望维度渐近达到其最大可能值。此外，还分析了星积维度的方差，提供了明确的渐近上界，并讨论了这些结果对私有信息检索、安全分布式矩阵乘法、量子纠错和密码分析的意义。

item.title: Surface code stabilizer measurements for Rydberg atoms
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Sven Jandura, Laura Pecorari, and Guido Pupillo
Author(s): Sven Jandura, Laura Pecorari, and Guido Pupillo Optimizing quantum gates for logical errors—rather than solely focusing on physical fidelity—is found to improve surface code performance in neutral-atom arrays. Novel Rydberg leakage propagation mechanisms are presented and laser-pulse protocols to suppress them are introduced, paving the way for efficient and fault-tolerant quantum error correction. [Phys. Rev. Research 8, L022059] Published Fri Jun 26, 2026

item.title: A context-aware gate set tomography: Improving the self-consistent characterization of trapped-ion universal gate sets by leveraging non-Markovianity
item.description:
📚 npj Quantum Information
👤 P. Viñas, A. Bermudez
npj Quantum Information, Published online: 26 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41534-026-01313-z A context-aware gate set tomography: Improving the self-consistent characterization of trapped-ion universal gate sets by leveraging non-Markovianity

item.title: Theory uncertainties in predictions of strong-field QED
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 T. G. Blackburn
Author(s): T. G. Blackburn Experiments using high-power lasers and relativistic electron beams will soon be capable of precision testing of the theory of strong-field quantum electrodynamics. The comparison between experiment and theory always occurs via numerical simulations, but the lack of quantitative uncertainty bounds f… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023346] Published Fri Jun 26, 2026

item.title: Criterion for qubit-assisted quantum metrology approaching Heisenberg scaling
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Peng Chen, Jun Jing
arXiv:2606.26167v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study in this work the metrology precision of a probe system coupled to an ancillary qubit. Restricting the probe-qubit coupling along only one or two directions is found to be a sufficient criterion for the effective dynamical generator to achieve the Heisenberg limit in precision. Under the criterion, the quantum Fisher information (QFI) about the to-be-estimated parameter becomes the expectation value of mean square of the effective generator with respect to the initial state of the composite system. Our criterion is justified in two distinct systems. For a bosonic probe, QFI about the displacement estimation is found to be proportional to the mean excitation number of probe. It renders a counterintuitive result that quantum metrology…

item.title: Measures of Chirality in Mixed-State Topological Phases
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Shijun Sun, Bader Aldossari, Rasmit Devkota, Zhu-Xi Luo
arXiv:2606.26235v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: What does it mean for a mixed-state topological phase to be chiral? Mathematically, chirality can be sharply characterized through the symmetry algebra of the mixed state. Physically, however, the question is far more subtle. In pure states, chirality in topological phase is tied to a web of familiar diagnostics, involving bulk-boundary correspondence, a gapless entanglement spectrum, a nontrivial modular commutator, and a quantized thermal Hall response. We show that none of these diagnostics remain reliable in mixed states. Instead, for decohered topological phases with a known error-free parent state, we propose two relative-entropy-based measures that can diagnose chirality, with one of them further extracting the chiral central charge.…

item.title: Satellite-assisted entanglement distribution with high-dimensional photonic encoding
item.description:
📚 Quantum Science and Technology
👤 V Domínguez Tubío, M C Dijksman and J Borregaard
Satellite-assisted entanglement distribution is a promising approach for realizing long-range quantum networking. However, the limited coherence time of existing quantum memories makes it challenging to obtain multiple event-ready entangled pairs between ground stations since one pair decoheres before the successful distribution of another. We demonstrate how this can be circumvented by pairing existing satellite-compatible spontaneous parametric down conversion (SPDC) sources with qudit-compatible quantum memories on ground. By operating the SPDC source as a source of time-bin encoded photonic qudits, simultaneous distribution of multiple entangled pairs between the ground stations can be achieved at a significantly higher rate than if the SPDC sources was operated as a source of photoni…

item.title: Dissipative Quantum Multiplicative Weights with Sampling Feedback: A Classically Hard Primitive Realized via Engineered Open-System Dynamics
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Agung Trisetyarso, Lenny Putri Yulianti, Kridanto Surendro
arXiv:2606.26162v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce emph{Dissipative Quantum Multiplicative Weights with Sampling Feedback} (DQMW-Sample), an online-learning primitive in which engineered open quantum-system dynamics prepare a Gibbs state whose computational-basis measurement supplies the loss feedback. The central conceptual contribution is to lift the computational hardness of constant-temperature Gibbs sampling into a physically realizable online-learning primitive. By engineering a Davies-type dissipator whose per-round feedback cannot be efficiently simulated classically, we obtain a learning-theoretic separation in which DQMW-Sample achieves asymptotically sublinear regret while every efficient classical learner suffers constant average regret on a suitably constructed inst…

item.title: Interpretable rule-based learning in an autonomous thermodynamic network
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sparrow Suderman, Giulia Rubino
arXiv:2606.26220v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine learning is typically described in terms of deterministic logical operations, whereas physical systems generally operate in the presence of noise, dissipation and irreversibility. Here, we turn these physical effects into computational resources for an autonomous, interpretable learning architecture. We develop a classifier based on thermodynamic neurons, which are autonomous quantum thermal machines that implement logical operations through heat flow, and use these to construct a stochastic version of the Tsetlin machine, an interpretable rule-based learning architecture. By combining thermodynamic AND, NOT and OR gates with an autonomous coupling mechanism, we realise a learning system whose computation unfolds without the need for…

item.title: A fidelity metric for quantum annealing benchmarked by extreme scaling quantum Monte-Carlo simulations
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Gabriel Gouraud, Miha Srdinsek, Xavier Waintal
arXiv:2606.26233v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantum annealers are supposed to follow adiabatically the ground state of a system as its Hamiltonian slowly interpolates between a trivial phase and a non-trivial one; the non-trivial ground state being the solution to an optimization problem. Overwhelmingly, their performances are measured in terms of how well or fast the optimization problem is solved. While pragmatic, this approach is inherently brittle as it strongly depends on the problem considered and the classical algorithm used as the reference benchmark. Here, we propose a quantity that not only measures the end result but also the quality of the actual quantum annealing process itself. Our metric is the quantum annealing counterpart of the fidelity-per gate of gate-based quantum…

item.title: Permutation asymmetry unlocks emergent advantage in randomized Bell tests
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Wieslaw Laskowski, Tamas Vertesi, Jan Wojcik
arXiv:2606.26242v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: All maximally entangled two-qubit states violate local realism with the same probability under uniformly random projective measurements, yet they need not behave identically in randomized Bell tests. We show that when measurement settings are exchanged between the parties in sequential Bell experiments, permutation symmetry of the shared state determines the statistical relation between the two realizations. Permutationally invariant states yield identical nonlocality outcomes in both experiments, whereas asymmetric states can violate local realism in one realization but not in the other. This distinction leads to two operational consequences. First, it enables the detection of correlations between the measurement choices of Alice and Bob th…

item.title: Mechanisms governing photon-pair generation and emission directionality in quantum metasurfaces
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Alberto Paniate, Ivano Ruo-Berchera, Francesco Monticone
arXiv:2606.26303v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Metasurfaces are emerging as a promising platform for photon-pair generation through spontaneous parametric down-conversion, thanks to their compactness, integrability, and intrinsic multifunctionality, which enables the engineering of complex quantum states. However, their full potential remains only partially exploited because the physical mechanisms governing key properties of the generated photon pairs, such as generation efficiency and emission directionality, are not yet fully understood. As a result, metasurface designs and experimental configurations are often optimized through trial-and-error procedures. Here, we theoretically investigate the main mechanisms that control photon-pair generation and detection by studying how different…

item.title: Tailor Made Embeddings for Quantum Machine Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Aldo Lamarre, Dominik Šafránek
arXiv:2606.26312v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autoencoders transformed classical machine learning by solving the curse of dimensionality, enabling principled weight initialization and learning compact, structured representations. In this work, we extend this paradigm to quantum machine learning by introducing a variational autoencoder framework that learns task-specific quantum embeddings of classical data. We demonstrate that high-dimensional datasets, including ImageNet, can be compressed into a 13-qubit quantum representation while remaining reconstructable through a learned decoder. On MNIST (3 vs 5), our approach achieves 98.5% validation accuracy using a circuit-centric quantum classifier, within 1.2 percentage points of a classical neural network baseline (99.7%) and more than 30…

item.title: Improving device-independent quantum key distribution protocols through multiple routed Bell tests
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sujan Vijayaraj, Mauro Paternostro
arXiv:2606.26329v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Device-independent quantum key distribution (DI-QKD) offers security with the smallest possible set of assumptions about the experimental setup. The challenge posed by its implementation could be tackled using routed Bell tests with entanglement swapping, or distant Bell state measurement (BSM) units. However, practical distances still require local tests with close-to-ideal violations. We propose a DI-QKD protocol based on multiple sources and measurement devices where, in each round, routed tests are performed on randomly selected local devices. The violation of local Bell tests is checked even when a successful BSM projection is achieved. By requiring that such conditional tests remain consistent with the overall one, we achieve improveme…

item.title: Algebraic structures of the Lindblad equation
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Leonel Bixano, Guillermo López-Alvarez, Victor Alberto Cruz-Barriguete, V. G. Ibarra-Sierra, José Luis Cardoso, Juan Carlos Sandoval-Santana, Alejandro Kunold
arXiv:2606.26477v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We investigate the algebraic structure underlying the Lindblad equation for finite-dimensional open quantum systems. By introducing a suitable operator representation of the Liouville superoperator, we show that the dynamics can be formulated in terms of a closed algebra of Hermitian operators that is independent of the particular physical model. This formulation reveals that dissipative dynamics requires a substantially richer algebraic structure than purely unitary evolution, thereby providing a clear characterization of the additional complexity introduced by the Lindbladian. The resulting framework naturally leads to parametrizations of the dynamical map and to differential equations governing its evolution. We further derive recursion r…

item.title: Graph Structures for Local Distinguishability of Quantum Product States
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sooyeong Kim, David W. Kribs, Michael Nathanson, Rajesh Pereira, Sarah Plosker
arXiv:2606.26558v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We consider the problem of distinguishing sets of quantum product states with local operations and classical communication (LOCC). Recent work has used graph theory to identify sets of product states distinguishable with one-way LOCC. We extend these efforts to full two-way LOCC, with the first significant analysis of the set of graphs corresponding to bipartite product states that can be distinguished with two-way protocols after finitely many steps. We derive basic closure properties of the set of distinguishable graphs and identify some classes of graphs that guarantee local distinguishability and some graphs that do not. We also include several examples and forward-looking comments.

item.title: Quantum Fast-Forwarding Beyond Reversibility: The α-Perturbed n-Cycle
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Avah Banerjee, Asim Sharma, Sooraj Sooman
arXiv:2606.26584v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantum fast-forwarding (QFF) is usually formulated for reversible Markov chains, where the projected quantum walk evolution is exactly governed by Chebyshev polynomials of a Hermitian discriminant matrix. We study whether this framework can be extended to nonreversible dynamics for an α-perturbed n-cycle Markov chain, which preserves circulant structure while introducing controlled irreversibility. We show that the nonreversible case has a fundamental obstruction: for α ≠ 0, the eigenvalues of P_α leave the interval [-1,1], so Tₘ₍P_α) is not uniformly bounded and cannot arise as an exact unitary compression for all times. Thus, exact Chebyshev-based QFF does not extend directly beyond reversibility. Nevertheless, we obtain a finite-time app…

item.title: Nonadiabatic Holonomic Single-Qubit Gates in Non-Hermitian Systems
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Wei Li, Yue Zhang, Yu Kun Chen, Jia Yao Liang
arXiv:2606.26798v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Holonomic quantum computation offers a promising route to robust quantum gates, but decoherence remains a central obstacle in realistic implementations. Here we develop a nonadiabatic holonomic scheme for a driven three-level system in the no-jump regime described by an effective non-Hermitian Hamiltonian. Within a biorthogonal framework, tailored complex pulses enforce exact closure of the computational-subspace evolution at the final time despite the underlying nonunitary dynamics, enabling arbitrary single-qubit holonomic gates without requiring cyclic evolution in its orthogonal complement. In contrast to existing non-Hermitian treatments, which either neglect the overall exponential prefactor or, in adiabatic settings, include dissipati…

item.title: Dissipation-Induced Deviations from Kibble-Zurek Scaling in Non-Hermitian Quantum Annealing
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 H. Najafzadeh, S. Sadeghizade, R. Jafari, A. Langari
arXiv:2606.26870v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We revisit the quantum annealing problem in the non-Hermitian transverse-field Ising model. We determine, both analytically and numerically, the intrinsic transition probabilities and the resulting defect density. Our results reveal that, unlike the Hermitian case where defect production is dominated by modes near the gap-closing point, the non-Hermitian dynamics involve significant contributions from broad momentum sectors. We find that, depending on the dissipation strength, the defect density exhibits standard Kibble-Zurek scaling, anti-Kibble-Zurek behavior, and a suppression faster than the Kibble-Zurek prediction. We demonstrate that these deviations from the standard Kibble-Zurek scaling can be understood in terms of the underlying ex…

item.title: Scalable Message-Passing Quantum Graph Neural Networks in the Weisfeiler-Leman Hierarchy
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Snehal Raj, Brian Coyle, Léo Monbroussou, André J. Ferreira-Martins, Renato M. S. Farias, Elham Kashefi
arXiv:2606.26873v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graphs provide a natural language for relational data in chemistry, biology and optimisation. Graph neural networks (GNNs) have driven much of the recent progress in learning from such data through message passing, a single primitive that generalises convolution and attention. Quantum counterparts have been proposed, but with limited connection to message passing and few guarantees on performance or scalability. More broadly, the trainability of variational quantum circuits is a recognised bottleneck for their wide applicability, and pre-training has emerged as one way to address it. Yet for a quantum model to be useful, it must offer expressivity guarantees along with demonstrable scalability. Here we show how a quantum graph neural network…

item.title: Shallow Quantum Circuits for Deep Chemistry via Valence Bond Embeddings
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Francisco Javier del Arco Santos, Jakob S. Kottmann
arXiv:2606.26882v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantum chemistry is one of the major potential applications in quantum computation. Currently there is a considerable focus on relatively small active spaces as a consequence of hardware noise and exponential bottlenecks in simulations. In the long run, there will be an increasing demand in reliable approximations for larger systems -- both, as initial states for projective algorithms like the quantum phase estimation or for the evaluation of dynamical properties. While numerous approaches to select active spaces and extrapolate basis set accuracy exist, there is currently no consistent approach that results in a single quantum circuit for the total system. In this work, we combine hybrid Fermionic-Bosonic encodings with the structured appr…

item.title: On the simple derivation of the Casimir effect
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hai-Chau Nguyen, Matthias Kleinmann
arXiv:2606.26946v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Casimir effect in its simplest form describes the attraction of two parallel conducting plates at close distance due to the vacuum fluctuation of the electromagnetic field. Its derivation can be found in many introductory works on quantum optics. Here we return to the original paper by Casimir and find subtle nuances in his derivation that are worth discussing to give a complete picture of a mathematically sound derivation of the effect.

item.title: Lattice patch structure for fixed-frequency transmon quantum computer with high-fidelity CNOT gates
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Chanpyo Kim, Jeongsoo Kang, Younghun Kwon
arXiv:2606.27017v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Superconducting transmon processors represent a leading platform for large-scale quantum computing due to their high gate fidelities and scalability. However, conventional qubit-coupler-qubit (QCQ) architectures face critical physical and structural bottlenecks, notably frequency crowding [spectator qubit collisions] during system scaling and inefficient mapping onto the standard surface code.To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel lattice-patch architecture that couples four fixed-frequency transmons to a single fixed-frequency coupler.This design enhances qubit connectivity and maps directly onto the surface-code lattice unit [plaquette], thereby minimizing the compilation overhead associated with logical qubit implementation. Fu…

item.title: Efficient foundation decoders for fault-tolerant quantum computing
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ge Yan, Shanchuan Li, Shiyi Xiao, Pengyue Ma, Hanyan Cao, Feng Pan, Yuxuan Du
arXiv:2606.27119v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Foundation decoders, a class of high-capacity neural decoders, are leading candidates for fault-tolerant quantum computing, with accurate and efficient decoding at large code distances. However, their construction often faces a steep scaling barrier, as larger code distances rapidly amplify the cost of syndrome generation and neural optimization. To address this bottleneck, here we devise neural transfer unification (NTU), a unified framework for efficient foundation decoders. A central feature of NTU is its ability to align decoding tasks across code distances via algebraic structures shared by scalable code families, which enables knowledge learned on smaller codes to accelerate large-scale decoder training. We instantiate NTU as NTU-Trans…

item.title: Rate-2/3 Girth-8 (3,18)-Regular Quantum LDPC Codes from Two-Branch Finite-Field Bases and CPM Lifts
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Koki Okada, Kenta Kasai
arXiv:2606.27130v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We construct a rate-2/3 quantum low-density parity-check (LDPC) code from a (3,18)-regular two-branch finite-field base and a circulant-permutation-matrix (CPM) lift of degree P=101. The resulting code is a Calderbank-Shor-Steane (CSS) code with parameters [[34542,23032,dłe 310]]. We do not regard this upper bound as an estimate of the true minimum distance; rather, dłe310 is the tightest upper bound currently obtained from structural lifts and decoder-produced logical errors. The construction has row weight 18 and column weight 3, and the Tanner graphs of H_X and H_Z separately have girth 8. Decoder experiments with log-likelihood-ratio (LLR) joint belief propagation (BP) and deterministic post-processing show no failures in 10⁸ trials at p…

item.title: From Approximate Floquet Engineering to Full Floquet Theory: Coherent Control of Chiral Spin Systems in Spintronics
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Andrea Simion, Claudiu Filip, Coriolan Tiusan
arXiv:2606.27183v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Coherent control of interacting spin systems under time-periodic driving is a central challenge in spin-based quantum technologies. Here we demonstrate the applicability of a full Floquet-space formalism, adapted from Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) methodologies, to model the dynamics of driven coupled electron spins in the presence of a static magnetic field B0 and a transverse oscillating field B1. The framework explicitly includes isotropic exchange coupling J and the chiral Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya antisymmetric exchange interaction (DMI), and its numerical convergence is systematically validated with respect to Fourier-space truncation. In the non-interacting limit, the expected driven-spin dynamics is recovered, with the oscillation per…

item.title: Quantum group codes for non-Clifford logic: enhanced decoding, addressability and parallelizability
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jean Gasnier, Virgile Guémard
arXiv:2606.27211v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce a framework based on classical quasi group codes to define a class of quantum CSS codes, called quantum group codes, supporting transversal multi-control-Z gates which are both addressable and parallelizable, thus allowing to efficiently implement circuits composed of non-Clifford gates at the logical level. Building on this, we use a lifting procedure of classical AG codes established from class field theory to construct good quantum group codes with improved decoding complexity and logical multi-control-Z gate parallelizability. More precisely, on input a good quantum AG code over the alphabet mathbb F_q with transversal mathsfC^mmathsf Z gate, we apply this lifting procedure to its underlying classical AG code and obtain a qu…

item.title: A 0.651-approximation to quantum Max Cut via Rydberg atoms
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Tomás Crosta, Matthieu Saubanere, Felix Huber
arXiv:2606.27224v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantum Max Cut, also known as the anti-ferromagnetic Heisenberg Hamiltonian, is a QMA-complete problem which serves as a benchmark for approximation algorithms in quantum physics. Here we develop a hybrid approximation algorithm to quantum Max Cut, which uses the natural quantum dynamics of Rydberg atom systems in combination with semidefinite programming and randomized rounding. It achieves a conditional approximation ratio of 0.651, compared to the best-known ratio of 0.614 that relies on semidefinite programming alone. The algorithm is robust in the sense that the advantage persists even if the annealing procedure of the Rydberg atom system obtains a state whose energy is only 89% of its true ground state energy. Our approach opens a new…

item.title: A hardware-safety-gated system for LLM-written native ARTIQ control code on a trapped-ion platform
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Duanyang Wang, Lu Qi, Yuanheng Xie, Norbert M. Linke, Kenneth R. Brown
arXiv:2606.27231v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large-language-model (LLM) agents can write and run experimental control code. This allows laboratory work to be conducted autonomously. However, this autonomy raises a safety problem that prior work has not addressed. Unchecked code can damage the apparatus, and there is no formal, per-operation boundary between human authorization/supervision, and agent decisions. We present a control system that places an LLM agent in the loop of a trapped-ion experiment while enforcing such a boundary. The agent controls the existing Advanced Real-Time Infrastructure for Quantum physics (ARTIQ) stack through tools provided by a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. No tool call reaches the hardware unless it carries an authorization token bound to its exa…

item.title: Cultivating logical catalysts for fault-tolerant dyadic phase rotations
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yichen Xu, Xiao Wang
arXiv:2606.27358v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce a surface-code cultivation protocol for reusable logical catalyst states that implement exact fine dyadic phase gates Z²⁻b by phase kickback. The catalyst is an eigenstate of a high-period Clifford circuit U, with a direct construction supported on O(2^b) logical qubits. Once cultivated, each invocation implements the target phase through a controlled-U gadget, removing Clifford+T synthesis approximation error from the online gate and making the online non-Clifford depth independent of the target logical accuracy. As a concrete demonstration, we construct a catalyst for sqrtT=Z¹/⁸, where U is a nine-qubit brickwork Clifford circuit and controlled-U consists of eight controlled-CNOTs. Starting from nine distance-three rotated-sur…

item.title: Giant Second-Harmonic Generation in 3R-MoS2/MLM Hybrid Metasurfaces Cavities
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Omar A. M. Abdelraouf
arXiv:2606.26751v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Nonlinear 2D materials such as 3R-phase molybdenum disulfide (3R-MoS2) offer strong second-order optical nonlinearities in an atomically thin platform, making them attractive for on-chip frequency conversion, quantum light generation, and integrated nonlinear nanophotonics. However, the second harmonic generation (SHG) efficiency of monolayer or few-layer 3R-MoS2 deposited on planar substrates remains fundamentally limited by weak light-matter interaction, poor phase matching, and small interaction volumes. Here, we introduce NanoPhotoNet-PINL, a physics informed AI-driven inverse design framework based on a hybrid one-dimensional convolutional neural network and deep neural network autoencoder, tailored for nonlinear MLMs metasurfaces. Th…

item.title: An Iterative Dual-Channel Neural Quantum State Algorithm for Selected Configuration Interaction
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jen-Yu Chang, Yi-Chun Chang, Yu-Jui Lin, Ming-Chun Yang, Hsiu-Chi Tsai, Tai-Yue Li, Nan Yow Chen, Tsung-Wei Huang, En-Jui Kuo
arXiv:2606.26760v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Accurately solving the electronic Schrödinger equation for strongly correlated systems remains a central challenge in quantum chemistry, where the exponential growth of configuration space limits the applicability of exact methods. Selected Configuration Interaction (SCI) algorithms address this challenge by adaptively constructing compact determinantal expansions, yet their efficiency depends critically on the quality of the sampling strategy used to identify chemically important configurations. Here we introduce the Handover Iterative Neural Quantum State (HI-NQS) algorithm, which embeds a classically trained autoregressive Transformer neural quantum state within the iterative sample--diagonalize--update framework of Sample-Based Quantum…

item.title: Quantum Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Solving Integro and Fractional PDEs
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Deepak Gupta, Ratikanta Behera
arXiv:2606.26865v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Quantum neural networks have emerged as powerful models for approximating nonlinear functions. Yet their use in solving integro-differential equations (IDEs) and fractional integro-partial differential equations (FIPDEs), which involve inherently nonlocal operators, remains unexplored. This work introduces a quantum physics-informed neural network (QPINN) framework that combines a quantum neural network with the governing equations of general nonlinear IDEs and FIPDEs. The proposed quantum network uses an affine feature map and variational quantum circuits to produce trial solutions with explicit trigonometric structure. We prove a quantitative L²(μ) universal approximation theorem for this architecture, achieving a convergence rate of O(n…

item.title: Bipartite entanglement of the primordial Majorana during inflation
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ai-chen Li, Han-Qing Shi, Keyun Wu
arXiv:2606.26869v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We use a primordial Majorana field as a fermionic probe of quantum correlations during inflation. Working in a torsion-free FLRW spacetime, we derive the two-component Majorana mode equations in an axion-inflation background and construct the corresponding quadratic Hamiltonian in the paired momentum basis. Hamiltonian diagonalization and the fermionic squeezing formalism are shown to give the same Bogoliubov transformation, providing a direct map from the Majorana mode functions to the instantaneous occupation number and to the two-mode state of each (boldsymbolk,-boldsymbolk) pair. Because Fermi statistics restricts each helicity sector to the vacuum and one-pair states, the resulting Hilbert space is finite and the bipartite quantum-inf…

item.title: Observation of Non-Hermitian Skin Dynamics in the Liouvillian Regime
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Shu Yang, Yeyang Sun, Lingrui Hong, Yi Yang
arXiv:2606.27043v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Open quantum systems generally do not perfectly preserve phase coherence: coupling to uncontrolled environments requires a density-matrix description based on the Liouvillian framework beyond pure-state wave evolution. Realizing and probing such dynamics in a programmable platform is therefore essential for connecting coherent physics to realistic dissipative settings. Here we implement a tunable open-system quantum walk in a photonic mesh lattice, where controlled phase noise produces adjustable dephasing and non-reciprocal gain-loss imbalance provides an independently tunable non-Hermitian drive. This allows us to continuously interpolate between coherent quantum walks and incoherent classical walks, and to observe how directional transp…

item.title: Constant Runtime Error Mitigation via Restricted Evolution
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Gaurav Saxena, Thi Ha Kyaw
arXiv:2409.06636v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Error mitigation techniques, while instrumental in extending the capabilities of near-term quantum computers, often suffer from exponential resource scaling with noise levels. To address this limitation, we introduce a novel approach, namely, constant runtime error mitigation by restricted evolution (EMRE). Through numerical simulations, we demonstrate that EMRE surpasses the performance of Probabilistic Error Cancellation (PEC) while maintaining constant sampling overhead. The constant sampling overhead comes at the cost of a small non-zero bias. We provide a methodology to compute the optimal bias by connecting it to a resource-theoretic measure. We also evaluate bounds on the bias under different noise models and give exact results fo…

item.title: Towards Unconditional Uncloneable Encryption
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Pierre Botteron, Anne Broadbent, Eric Culf, Ion Nechita, Clément Pellegrini, Denis Rochette
arXiv:2410.23064v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Uncloneable encryption is a cryptographic primitive which encrypts a classical message into a quantum ciphertext, such that two quantum adversaries are limited in their capacity of being able to simultaneously decrypt, given the key and quantum side-information produced from the ciphertext. Since its initial proposal and scheme in the random oracle model by Broadbent and Lord [TQC 2020], uncloneable encryption has developed into an important primitive at the foundation of quantum uncloneability for cryptographic primitives. Despite sustained efforts, however, the question of unconditional uncloneable encryption (and in particular of the simplest case, called an uncloneable bit) has remained elusive. Here, we propose a candidate for the u…

item.title: The exact convex roof for GHZ-W mixtures for three qubits and beyond
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Andreas Osterloh
arXiv:2501.07084v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: I present an exact solution for the convex roof of the square root of the threetangle for rank two density matrices and for all states within the Bloch sphere. Aside the formerly known two tetrahedra it contains three additional optimal tetrahedra and connecting triangular optimal decompositions. The remaining optimal decompositions are one-dimensional. Optimal decompositions are proved to contain as many states from the zero-polytope as possible, a property that is called zero-state locking; it will be the working horse throughout this work. In addition, an inequality is derived which decides about the optimality of the decompositions under consideration. The footprint of the measure of entanglement consists in a characteristic pattern…

item.title: Non-stabilizerness and violations of CHSH inequalities
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Stefano Cusumano, Lorenzo Campos Venuti, Simone Cepollaro, Immacolata De Simone, Gianluca Esposito, Daniele Iannotti, Barbara Jasser, Jovan Odavić, Michele Viscardi, Alioscia Hamma
arXiv:2504.03351v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We study quantitatively the interplay between entanglement and non-stabilizer resources in violating the CHSH inequalities. We show that, while non-stabilizer resources are necessary, they must have a specific structure, namely they need to be both asymmetric and (surprisingly) it local. We employ stabilizer entropy (SE) to quantify the non-stabilizer resources involved and the probability of violation given the resources. We show how spectral quantities related to the flatness of entanglement spectrum and its relationship with non-local SE affect the CHSH inequality. Finally, we utilize these results - together with tools from representation theory - to construct a systematic way of building ensembles of states with higher probability o…

item.title: A complexity theory for non-local quantum computation
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Andreas Bluhm, Simon Höfer, Alex May, Mikka Stasiuk, Philip Verduyn Lunel, Henry Yuen
arXiv:2505.23893v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Non-local quantum computation (NLQC) replaces a local interaction between two systems with a single round of communication and shared entanglement. Despite many partial results, it is known that a characterization of entanglement cost in at least certain NLQC tasks would imply significant breakthroughs in complexity theory. Here, we avoid these obstructions and take an indirect approach to understanding resource requirements in NLQC, which mimics the approach used by complexity theorists: we study the relative hardness of different NLQC tasks by identifying resource efficient reductions between them. Most significantly, we prove that f-measure and f-route, the two best studied NLQC tasks, are in fact equivalent under O(1) overhead reduct…

item.title: Role of overparametrization in quantum approximate optimization
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Daniil Rabinovich, Andrey Kardashin, Soumik Adhikary
arXiv:2508.10086v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Variational quantum algorithms have emerged as a cornerstone of contemporary quantum algorithms research. While they have demonstrated considerable promise in solving problems of practical interest, efficiently determining the minimal quantum resources necessary to obtain such a solution remains an open question. In this work, inspired by concepts from classical machine learning, we investigate the impact of overparameterization on the performance of variational algorithms. Our study focuses on the quantum approximate optimization algorithm (QAOA) -- a prominent variational quantum algorithm designed to solve combinatorial optimization problems. We investigate if circuit overparametrization is necessary and sufficient to solve such probl…

item.title: Efficient learning of bosonic Gaussian unitaries
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Marco Fanizza, Vishnu Iyer, Junseo Lee, Antonio A. Mele, Francesco A. Mele
arXiv:2510.05531v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Bosonic Gaussian unitaries are fundamental building blocks of central continuous-variable quantum technologies such as quantum-optic interferometry and bosonic error-correction schemes. In this work, we present the first time-efficient algorithm for learning bosonic Gaussian unitaries with a rigorous analysis. Our algorithm produces an estimate of the unknown unitary that is accurate to small worst-case error, measured by the physically motivated energy-constrained diamond distance. Its runtime and query complexity scale polynomially with the number of modes, the inverse target accuracy, and natural energy parameters quantifying the allowed input energy and the unitary's output-energy growth. The protocol uses only experimentally friendl…

item.title: Theory and Architecture of Syndrome-Resolved Logical Gates
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Nobuyuki Yoshioka, Alireza Seif, Peter Groszkowski, Andrew Cross, Ali Javadi-Abhari
arXiv:2510.08290v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We introduce a general framework for weak transversal gates, namely syndrome-resolved implementation of logical unitaries realized by local physical unitaries, and demonstrate its wide impact in three applications: partial fault-tolerant architecture, short-depth state preparation, and magic state distillation. Our theoretical contribution is to prove a sufficient condition for a general Calderbank-Shor-Steane code to admit weak transversal gates, and to present an efficient algorithm to determine the physical multiqubit Pauli rotations. To demonstrate the practicality of weak transversal gates in the near future, we propose a partially fault-tolerant Clifford+φ architecture that implements in-place Pauli rotations via a repeat-until-suc…

item.title: Probing Qubit Noise with a Channel-Resolved Post-Markovian Master Equation
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Chun-Tse Li, Jingming Tan, Vasil Gucev, Daniel A. Lidar
arXiv:2510.12894v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Accurate noise characterization is essential for scaling quantum processors toward fault-tolerant operation. Although reduced qubit dynamics are often modeled with Markovian master equations, present-day devices can exhibit memory effects generated by residual qubit-qubit couplings, structured environments, and finite bath correlation times. Here we develop a channel-resolved, Post-Markovian Master Equation model for non-Markovian noise and test it in superconducting qubits. Using idle-evolution tomography on IBM Quantum processors, we identify complementary operational signatures of non-Markovianity, including violations of CP-divisibility and revivals of distinguishability-based information-backflow measures. We further derive a closed…

item.title: Gleason's Theorem for a Qubit as Part of a Composite System
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Vincenzo Fiorentino, Stefan Weigert
arXiv:2511.15607v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We extend Gleason's theorem to the two-dimensional Hilbert space of a qubit by invoking the standard axiom that describes composite quantum systems. The tensor-product structure allows us to derive density matrices and Born's rule for d=2 from a simple requirement: the probabilities assigned to measurement outcomes must not depend on whether a system is considered on its own or as a subsystem of a larger one. In line with Gleason's original theorem, our approach assigns probabilities only to projection-valued measures, while other known extensions rely on considering more general classes of measurements. This extension of Gleason's theorem to two-dimensional systems is shown to remain valid for some foil theories of quantum theory.

item.title: Simulating Gaussian boson sampling on graphs in polynomial time
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Konrad Anand, Zongchen Chen, Mary Cryan, Graham Freifeld, Leslie Ann Goldberg, Heng Guo, Xinyuan Zhang
arXiv:2511.16558v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We show that a distribution related to Gaussian Boson Sampling (GBS) on graphs can be sampled classically in polynomial time. Graphical applications of GBS typically sample from this distribution, and thus quantum algorithms do not provide exponential speedup for these applications. We also show that another distribution related to Boson sampling can be sampled classically in polynomial time.

item.title: Performance enhancing of hybrid quantum-classical Benders approach for MILP optimization
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sergio López-Baños, Elisabeth Lobe, Ontje Lünsdorf, Oriol Raventós
arXiv:2601.14024v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Mixed-integer linear programming problems are extensively used in industry for a wide range of optimization tasks. However, as they get larger, they present computational challenges for classical solvers within practical time limits. Quantum annealers can, in principle, accelerate the solution of problems formulated as quadratic unconstrained binary optimization instances, but their limited scale currently prevents achieving practical speedups. Quantum-classical algorithms have been proposed to take advantage of both paradigms and to allow current quantum computers to be used in larger problems. In this work, a hardware-agnostic Benders' decomposition algorithm and a series of enhancements with the goal of taking the most advantage of qu…

item.title: A Complete Equational Presentation of Qudit Circuits via Polycontrolled PROPs
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Colin Blake
arXiv:2602.09873v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: High-dimensional quantum computation needs a native circuit-level equational theory for qudits. We give the first finite schematic equational theory that is sound and complete for exact unitary qudit circuits in every finite dimension at least two. Circuits are built from local gates, sequential and parallel composition, and value-controls; equality is derivable exactly when the standard unitary denotations agree. For each dimension, a finite list of local bounded-arity axiom schemata presents the theory, and the diagrammatic shapes do not depend on d. Primitive value-control makes control on a chosen basis value part of the language, so local rules generate the internal algebra of controlled operations within the circuit PROP. This give…

item.title: Macroscopic Quantum Electrodynamics with Gain: Modified Fluctuations and Their Consequences
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Daigo Oue
arXiv:2602.22429v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Macroscopic quantum electrodynamics (MQED) provides a unified framework for describing quantum electromagnetic fields in arbitrary macroscopic environments. Central to this theory is the field correlation, which governs both radiative (e.g., Lamb shifts and the Purcell effect) and mechanical phenomena, such as van der Waals and Casimir forces. In this tutorial, we provide an overview of MQED and its extension to active media, highlighting fluctuation-induced forces as manifestations of gain-modified field correlations.
